items.title: Reliance remains mum even as Trump announces ‘historic’ $300 billion U.S. refinery deal
items.summary: The company did not make any announcement neither informed anything to stock exchanges, fuelling speculations on the veracity of U.S. President Trump’s claims

items.title: LPG crisis LIVE: Centre directs States to prevent hoarding, black marketing of cylinders; India’s LPG production up 25% since March 8 order
items.summary: Wealthy nations pledge record release of emergency oil reserves; No need for panic booking, Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas said

items.title: BNP, Bradesco, Rabobank Named Among Raízen Top Creditors
items.summary: BNP Paribas, Banco Bradesco SA and Coöperatieve Rabobank are among the largest creditors of Brazilian sugar and ethanol company Raízen SA, which is seeking to restructure some $12.6 billion of debt out-of-court.

items.title: Oil shock, inflation pressures dampen RBI rate-cut hopes
items.summary: Economists believe India's policy interest rate cuts are unlikely to continue due to West Asia crisis-driven inflation risks and the fading of a favorable base effect. Sustained foreign outflows and a weakening rupee could also force the Reserve Bank of India to reconsider further easing, potentially even a hike.

items.title: India must make garments for all seasons: Giriraj Singh
items.summary: India sets its sights on transforming into a $350 billion textile powerhouse with $100 billion in exports by the fiscal year 2030-31. To achieve this ambitious target, the industry must elevate its operations to scale up production, enhance value, and streamline garment manufacturing year-round. Moreover, leveraging free trade agreements for market access is crucial.

items.title: Supreme Court seeks timeline for CBI probe into NCR housing projects
items.summary: The Supreme Court has issued a decisive order for the CBI to outline a clear timeline for its investigation into numerous real estate developments in the NCR. The court strongly stated that lingering delays would only intensify the suffering of homebuyers.

items.title: Middle East tensions: Indian insurers review marine cargo exposure, ICICI Lombard hikes war-risk premium
items.summary: Indian insurers are reviewing marine cargo exposures in the Persian Gulf, Strait of Hormuz, and Red Sea due to escalating geopolitical tensions. ICICI Lombard is assessing shipments and introducing a 0.25% war-risk premium for Gulf-linked cargo, while existing policies remain in force. This reassessment aims to manage risks and ensure compliance with war-risk provisions.

items.title: Public sector banks still ahead of private peers in credit growth
items.summary: Public sector banks (PSBs) outpaced private lenders in credit growth during the December quarter of FY26, with loan growth ranging from 17-28% year-on-year. This surge boosted PSBs' market share in loans to 54.4%, while private banks saw a slight decline. Improved asset quality and corporate lending revival fueled PSB momentum.

items.title: India to release product list for faster PN3 FDI approvals
items.summary: The amended foreign direct investment (FDI) policy guidelines have allowed proposals for investments from China and other land-border countries to include advanced battery components, rare earth permanent magnets, rare earth processing sectors, whose applications will be processed and decided within 60 days.

items.title: Daily domestic LPG output raised by 25%: Petroleum ministry official
items.summary: India's cooking gas production has risen 25 percent. Refineries are maximizing output to counter supply halts from the Gulf. This boost helps fill a gap caused by the Iran war impacting imports. Households are advised against panic booking. India's crude oil supply remains secure. Additional crude and LNG cargoes are en route to India.

items.title: West Asia conflict could hurt agri input availability: UPL Executive
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz are driving up agricultural input costs, with fertilizer prices already surging 50-80%. While shipping, insurance, and energy costs have climbed, immediate agrochemical availability for the upcoming kharif season remains stable due to pre-built inventories.

items.title: As govt moves to prevent domestic LPG hoarding, stock-out at stores with kitchens going electric
items.summary: Fears of cooking gas shortages have triggered panic buying of electric cooking appliances across India. Retailers are seeing sales surge, with stocks running out rapidly. Manufacturers are increasing production to meet the unexpected demand. Consumers are purchasing multiple units, indicating a shift towards electric cooking.

items.title: '$95 for 2 months' US agency predicts prolonged conflict
items.summary: Brent is up 27% since the start of the war as the Strait of Hormuz is effectively shut and some oil production in the region has been curtailed. "We make the assumption in our modelling that the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz will cause oil production in the Middle East (West Asia) to fall further in the coming weeks. We assume this shut-in production will gradually ease as transit through the Strait resumes," the EIA, the primary federal authority in the US for energy statistics, said in its short-term energy outlook.

items.title: FDI policy eased: Relaxed PN3 opens door for global funds with Chinese stakes
items.summary: In a strategic shift, India has relaxed its foreign investment regulations, facilitating easier entry for companies from the US, Taiwan, South Korea, and Europe that have minimal Chinese ownership.

items.title: Dubai corridor shut amid West Asia war; diamond traders remix 'rough' sources
items.summary: India's diamond exporters are boosting rough stone purchases from Antwerp and Africa. The West Asia conflict has disrupted the Dubai trade corridor. Demand for rare white and coloured diamonds is increasing. Investors are turning to these diamonds as a safe investment. India processes most of the world's polished diamonds.

items.title: Iran war impact: Govt mulling diversion of stranded cargo to new markets
items.summary: India is assessing the diversion of export cargoes stranded by the West Asia crisis to alternative markets. The commerce ministry is seeking details from goods exporters on rerouting options and has also reached out to services exporters to gauge potential risks to trade, including movement restrictions and technology reliance.

items.title: Centre holds back on fuel exports ban due to logistics, tax hurdles as West Asia conflict rages
items.summary: India is not banning fuel exports despite global supply issues. Logistical hurdles, contractual obligations with overseas buyers, and tax waivers for export-only refineries are key reasons. Reliance Industries and Nayara Energy are major exporters. China and Thailand have curbed their exports. India aims to avoid adding to global market turbulence. Domestic refiners prefer selling to state retailers for better returns.

items.title: India medical device shortage: Polypropylene price surge threatens syringes, IV bags amid Iran war
items.summary: India's medical devices industry faces a polypropylene shortage due to the Iran war, causing a sharp price increase of over Rs 55 per kg. This surge, coupled with a deepening gas supply crisis impacting syringe production, threatens small and medium enterprises and overall healthcare delivery.

items.title: Travel Risk: Domestic destinations to gain ground this summer
items.summary: Indian travellers are increasingly booking domestic holidays for the upcoming summer. Global uncertainties and rising travel costs are driving this trend. Destinations like Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, and the North East are seeing high demand. Travellers are exploring cooler weather and unique experiences. Emerging destinations like Jaisalmer are also gaining popularity. This shift indicates a strong summer for domestic tourism.

items.title: Food and beverage outlets near shutdown, unable to fulfil online orders amid LPG crisis
items.summary: India's food and beverages sector faces a severe LPG crisis. Supply disruptions from West Asia are forcing restaurants to close and halt deliveries. Many outlets are reducing menu items to conserve cooking gas. This situation impacts millions of livelihoods and the gig economy. Authorities are reviewing allocations, prioritizing domestic households.

items.title: Delhi HC seeks reply on Hero Moto's 'MotorHunk' plea
items.summary: The Delhi High Court is reviewing Hero MotoCorp's challenge to the 'MotorHunk' trademark. Hero MotoCorp claims the mark is too similar to its 'Hunk' brand, potentially confusing customers. Nitin Khanna, founder of MotorHunk, argues the marks are distinct and Hero MotoCorp no longer uses its 'Hunk' mark.

items.title: Chinese  smartphone companies OnePlus, Realme boost online play
items.summary: Chinese smartphone makers are moving sales online. OnePlus is telling retailers most stock will be online. Realme has cut its offline sales team. This is to manage rising component costs. Online sales are cheaper than offline. This helps brands offer competitive prices. This shift comes as handset sales are expected to fall.

items.title: Dollar rises, as investors remain on edge about Middle East risks
items.summary: GLOBAL-FOREX/ (UPDATE 5):FOREX-Dollar rises, as investors remain on edge about Middle East risks

items.title: JPMorgan Tees Up EA Debt Sale as Iran War Shakes Credit Markets
items.summary: As JPMorgan Chase & Co. kicked off investor discussions this week over the $20 billion financing for Electronic Arts Inc., nervousness around a protracted war in the Middle East cast a shadow over the largest ever debt sale for a leveraged buyout.

items.title: Jindal Steel bags another iron ore mine in Odisha
items.summary: The steelmaker won a state government auction for the Rengalaberha North-East Extension and Nuagan West iron ore block on Wednesday by offering a 111.15% premium over the base price, they said.

items.title: Private sector optimism rises to decade high on strong demand
items.summary: With the dawn of a new year on the horizon, Indian firms are brimming with optimism. Consumer demand remains robust, driving companies to invest significantly in innovative projects and R&D. Yet, the dark cloud of rising oil prices threatens to dampen this enthusiasm. Nonetheless, this surge of positivity is the strongest we've witnessed in ten years.

items.title: Wall St slips as escalating war in Iran sours risk appetite
items.summary: USA-STOCKS/ (UPDATE 3):US STOCKS-Wall St slips as escalating war in Iran sours risk appetite

items.title: Settlements drive surge in SEBI income to ₹2,713 crore in FY25
items.summary: Settlement and compounding receipts jump nearly eight-fold to ₹814.55 crore

items.title: LPG shortage? UP, Delhi, WB prepare fight vs hoarding & black marketing post-Centre's 'don't panic' call
items.summary: UP, Delhi and West Bengal are the first to prepare their action plans against LPG hoarding and black marketing as India assures the public of ample energy supplies and urges the public not to rush-book cooking gas refills in panic.

items.title: Sales of electric cooktops sizzle as LPG flameout worries families
items.summary: The conflict in West Asia has led to unprecedented demand for the product on e-commerce platforms and retail stores; the prices for induction cooktops have gone up but supplies largely remained available though the inventory is quickly going down

items.title: West Asia conflict: India hails IEA move to release emergency oil stocks
items.summary: New Delhi said it was closely monitoring the “evolving situation in global energy markets”, and stands ready to take “appropriate measures, as necessary”

items.title: India ready to support oil markets as IEA agrees record release
items.summary: India has pledged to support global oil markets by taking appropriate measures, aligning with the International Energy Agency's record 400 million barrel oil release. This move aims to stabilize prices that have surged due to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran, which has disrupted India's own energy supplies.

items.title: A revision of GDP and its implications
items.summary: The revised GDP series with 2022-23 as the base year shows a reduction in the absolute size of GDP and some changes in the production structure of the economy; while the correction in GDP size appears welcome, it remains unclear whether the revision addresses the red flags raised about the 2011-12 series

items.title: India’s LPG production up 25% since supply maintenance orders; officials also urge refraining from panic booking
items.summary: India imports 60% of its LPG requirements of which 90% is routed through the Strait of Hormuz

items.title: Uptake of induction stoves sluggish in low-income Indian households
items.summary: While a small minority of Indian households use induction cooktops, only 5% of Indian households had ‘electric penetration’ in their kitchens, say experts

items.title: Google Cloud completes $32 billion acquisition of cloud security firm Wiz — Here's what this means
items.summary: Google Cloud has completed acquisition of Wiz, enhancing cloud security capabilities. The deal allows the Alphabet owned unit to leverage Wiz's technology for multicloud cybersecurity. Here's all we know:

items.title: Middle East conflict: Get ready for oil to hit $200 a barrel, warns Iran as IRGC strikes Thai vessel in Hormuz Strait
items.summary: After peaking near $120 on Monday, oil prices have settled around $90, reflecting market hopes for a diplomatic breakthrough and the reopening of the waterway.

items.title: India has overall coal stock of 88 days, confident of meeting rise in power demand: Ministry
items.summary: Approximately 54.05 MT was already available at thermal power plants.

items.title: Iran‑Israel war: IndiGo to resume flights to nine Middle East destinations, select European routes from March 12
items.summary: IndiGo is restarting flights to nine Middle Eastern cities and some European destinations on March 12, 2026. The airline is working with authorities to ensure safe travel. Passengers should check flight status for any changes. IndiGo is contacting affected customers to arrange new travel plans. The airline is committed to assisting passengers during this evolving situation.

items.title: Eased FDI norms balance capital inflows and strategic safeguards: Experts
items.summary: India's relaxed FDI norms for land-bordering countries aim to attract capital and technology while safeguarding interests. Experts highlight the pragmatic balance achieved, with a 60-day expedited approval for specified sectors and clarity on beneficial ownership, fostering supply-chain integration and boosting manufacturing.

items.title: Trump announces Reliance’s investment in new US refinery, Mukesh Ambani firm stays silent
items.summary: Shares of India’s most valuable company dipped after President Donald Trump touted Reliance Industries’ involvement in a $300 billion US refinery, even as the company declined to confirm its role and experts questioned the deal’s scale.

items.title: Oil prices jump over 6% despite IEA move to release 400 million barrels of crude amid US-Iran war; Brent back above $90
items.summary: Crude oil prices soared despite the IEA's decision to release 400 million barrels from reserves to combat supply disruptions in the Middle East. Brent futures rose to $93, and WTI to $88.90, highlighting the ongoing volatility in oil markets amid escalating regional conflicts.

items.title: Charles Schwab’s direct crypto offering is coming soon. What to know.
items.summary: The company is forging ahead with plans to allow customers to directly hold Bitcoin and Ethereum even though the “fervor” around digital assets has waned.

items.title: John Deere unveils 130 HP tractor at ₹65 lakh
items.summary: This technologically superior tractor comes with connectivity solutions, 100% radial tires, Straight-Row Vehicle Guidance System and front hitch and front PTO application

items.title: Microsoft and retired military chiefs back AI company Anthropic in court fight against Pentagon
items.summary: Microsoft and retired military chiefs back AI company Anthropic in court fight against Pentagon

items.title: IAB Tech Lab releases CoMP protocol for AI content licensing
items.summary: The specification will remain open for public comment until April 9, 2026, allowing the industry to provide feedback and support wider adoption. IAB is the global digital advertising technical standard-setting body.

items.title: India imported over 18,800 MT of uranium for reactors between 2008-25: Govt
items.summary: Between 2008 and 2025, India embarked on a journey of nuclear energy expansion, importing significant amounts of uranium for its reactors. This development has catapulted nuclear power generation to new heights. The nation aspires to achieve a formidable 100 GW of nuclear capacity, showcasing projects in progress as well as innovative future plans that incorporate both domestic and global technologies.

items.title: Investment rule tweaks will benefit electronics and rare earth sector in India, says DPIIT Secretary
items.summary: Industry players and investment analysts, too, say the changes to Press Note 3 will encourage more capital to flow into India, promote more joint ventures, and encourage collaboration

items.title: CCI dismisses complaint against IndiGo, Air India over alleged market dominance
items.summary: India's fair trade regulator CCI has dismissed a complaint against IndiGo and Air India. The complaint alleged unfair cancellation charges and market dominance. CCI found no evidence of competition law violations. Refund systems are in place and terms are disclosed. The regulator also rejected a request for a joint investigation into a separate flight cancellation case.

items.title: FDI in rare earth magnets, advanced battery components from land border countries part of fast-track approval list
items.summary: While some FDI norms are being eased, broadly the processes for security and political clearances will remain in place, said DPIIT Secretary

items.title: India fully geared to meet coal demand surge amid Iran‑Israel war energy concerns
items.summary: India stands ready to tackle any unforeseen escalations in coal demand, with a remarkable inventory of around 210 million tonnes of coal — ample for nearly 88 days of energy production. The current scenario showcases a surplus, as coal output and supply surpass what is needed.

items.title: India’s crude oil supply is secure, assures government
items.summary: Two additional crude cargoes are already on the way and will arrive in the coming days, further strengthening the crude oil supply position

items.title: Gold, silver rates today: Comex gold drops $87/oz; silver falls $5 as US dollar strengthens
items.summary: Precious metals fell on 11 March as the US dollar gained, making commodities pricier. Gold futures dropped $80 to $5,012 per ounce, while silver fell $4.67 to $79.64. Rising energy prices and inflation concerns complicate potential Federal Reserve rate cuts amid ongoing Middle East tensions.

items.title: Centre approves procurement of 1.25 lakh tonnes of rabi crops in Telangana
items.summary: The procurement will be done under the PSS; the process will include the procurement of 29,860 tonnes of gram, 37,020 tonnes of urad, 55,285 tonnes of groundnut and 3,690 tonnes of sunflower

items.title: Bears tighten grip: Nifty slips to 10-month low as geopolitical jitters slam D-Street
items.summary: Sensex plunged 1,342.27 points or 1.72% to close at 76,863.71, while the Nifty50 shed 394.75 points or 1.63% to end the session at 23,866.85

items.title: First US refinery in 50 years planned in Texas; Trump thanks India’s Reliance for investment
items.summary: The refinery is planned in Brownsville, Texas, a key energy and logistics hub along the US Gulf Coast.

items.title: US stocks today: Dow Jones drops 500 points as oil prices surge despite IEA move to release reserves
items.summary: Wall Street indexes dropped as investors weighed inflation data and a record crude oil reserve release aimed at curbing soaring energy prices amid Middle East tensions. Despite the IEA's move, oil prices climbed, and equities slipped after Iran's military warned of potential price surges. Investors also pushed back interest rate cut expectations.

items.title: Low strategic stockpiles pose risk to economy, says S&P Global
items.summary: Nomura cuts growth forecast

items.title: IEA nations to counter West Asia crisis with mega oil release; India welcomes move
items.summary: IEA member nations have agreed to carry out their largest-ever oil stock release amid disruptions due to the Middle East conflict.

items.title: Apollo-led funds invest $500 million in Adani Energy Solutions unit
items.summary: Apollo invests $500 million in Adani Energy Solutions to refinance assets and support infrastructure growth in India’s power sector

items.title: Big update in IDFC First Bank Rs 590 crore fraud case
items.summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) has launched searches at 19 locations across Chandigarh and the Tricity region in connection with the alleged Rs 590 crore fraud at IDFC First Bank’s Chandigarh branch involving funds from Haryana government agencies. Investigators are tracing the money trail linked to shell companies, jewellery firms and real estate investments.

items.title: Air India, AI Express plan 58 flights including special services to West Asia for March 12
items.summary: Air India and Air India Express will operate 58 flights connecting India and West Asia on March 12. These flights include scheduled services and special operations to the UAE, Saudi Arabia, and Oman. Two special flights are planned for Riyadh. Air India Express will also run eight flights to and from Muscat.

items.title: HDFC ERGO Appoints Parthanil Ghosh as New MD, CEO
items.summary: Ghosh joined the Company in 2016 following the merger with L&T General Insurance and has been serving as the Executive Director since May 1, 2025

items.title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Thursday
items.summary: Indian stock markets, Sensex and Nifty, experienced a significant downturn on Wednesday. Both indices closed nearly 2% lower. This decline followed a surge in crude oil prices and heightened tensions in West Asia. Foreign fund outflows and selling in major bank stocks also contributed to the market's fall. Investors are now awaiting key inflation data from the US and India.

items.title: India's Russian oil buy surges 50 pc amid scramble to replace lost barrels
items.summary: India's crude oil imports from Russia surged by 50 percent in March, a compelling response to the instability wreaking havoc in the Middle East. With the urgency to secure alternative supplies, India is prioritizing its energy needs against the backdrop of geopolitical turmoil.

items.title: Pieter Elbers fails the India test
items.summary: Pieter Elbers was a much sought-after global executive who played a key role in developing Dutch carrier KLM and Schiphol as an international connector, and was popular among employees. Former CEO Ronojoy Dutta's tenure ended early because IndiGo risked losing the race to hire Elbers to a Middle Eastern carrier.

items.title: Toll burden on national highways in Gujarat surge 56.5% in 5 years to FY25; toll collection grows to ₹20,383 crore in this period
items.summary: Annual toll collections have climbed steadily from ₹3,179 crore in FY21 to ₹4,975 crore in FY25

items.title: Start-up salary hikes recover unevenly as AI talent commands premiums
items.summary: Recruitment firms say the overall compensation environment is stabilising, though the pace of salary growth varies widely depending on sector and role

items.title: West Asia conflict poses downside risk, India GDP growth seen at 7.1 pc in FY27: Crisil Intelligence
items.summary: India's economy faces risks from West Asia conflict affecting oil prices. Real GDP growth is expected at 7.1 percent in FY27. This growth will be supported by consumer spending and investment. Exports are also set to grow. Retail inflation may rise to 4.3 percent. The Reserve Bank of India is likely to maintain interest rates.

items.title: Jaishankar speaks with Iran, Russia, France, EU on West Asia crisis
items.summary: Jaishankar stresses the necessity of ongoing consultations to help restore stability to the region

items.title: Restaurants, hotels seek minimum assured allocation of LPG supplies
items.summary: Kumar Rajgopalan, CEO, Retailers Association of India, noted that a disruption in supply will impact small and medium-size players, which in turn could lead to the non-availability of food for students, working professionals, etc.

items.title: Malaika Arora launches lifestyle accessories brand Maejoy
items.summary: The brand is co-created with Exceed Entertainment and Myntra Jabong India Private Limited (MJIPL), the B2B wholesale entity of Myntra

items.title: ICAR to host 3-day global conference on women in agri-food system from Thursday
items.summary: The event will focus on sharing inspiring success stories of women leaders and scientists, integrating gender-sensitive approaches into agricultural policies and institutions, etc.

items.title: Government reviews measures to strengthen maritime capacity
items.summary: Chaired by Ports, Shipping and Waterways Minister Sarbananda Sonowal, the meeting brought together members of the National Shipping Board (NSB), industry leaders, maritime stakeholders and senior ministry officials to deliberate on operational issues and policy priorities affecting the sector, an official release said.

items.title: Ahmedabad Heatwave Alert: 70 traffic signals to stay shut for 4 hours; check out locations
items.summary: Ahmedabad Heatwave Alert: As part of the heat action plan, around 70 traffic signals across the city will remain closed for 4 hours. Here's a list of locations where the traffic signals will be closed between 12 PM and 4 PM:

items.title: Crude volatility puts pressure on electronics supply chain, makers brace for margin hit
items.summary: Key plastic materials such as Acrylonitrile Butadiene Styrene and polypropylene, derived from crude and widely used in device casings, have seen prices rise 45-50 per cent

items.title: Prioritising LPG production in refineries helps boost output by 25%
items.summary: 70% of India’s crude imports now routed outside Strait of Hormuz; energy supplies are secure, assures govt

items.title: India has been a great partner in maintaining stable oil rates: Sergio Gor
items.summary: Sergio Gor has described India as one of world's largest oil consumers and refiners.

items.title: Iran-Israel War: LPG shortage forces restaurants to improvise with electric, microwaves, and firewood across India
items.summary: The ongoing West Asia conflict has triggered a worrying scarcity of commercial LPG cylinders across India, leaving many businesses, especially eateries and food vendors, in a difficult position. As they scramble to adapt, some are shifting to alternative cooking solutions like induction plates or reverting to firewood, creating a palpable sense of unease among proprietors.

items.title: Sebi eases certification requirement for sales, non-core staff associated with Research Services
items.summary: Sebi relaxed certification norms for Persons Associated with Research Services (PARS), allowing sales staff, relationship managers and other non-research personnel to qualify through a lighter certification module.

items.title: Oracle shares surge 15% to five-week high after strong earnings, upbeat revenue guidance
items.summary: Oracle's shares rose 15% to $171.76 after strong Q3 results and an increased revenue forecast for fiscal 2027. Revenue grew 22% year-over-year, with significant gains in infrastructure and cloud applications. The firm plans job cuts and anticipates $90 billion in revenue for 2027.

items.title: West Asia War: Commercial LPG crisis paralyses hotel operations in Kerala
items.summary: The vibrant hospitality scene in Kerala is dimming as hotels and eateries grapple with an alarming shortage of commercial LPG. Operations are in jeopardy as many establishments struggle to procure enough cylinders to meet daily demands. This crisis eerily mirrors the hardships experienced during the COVID-19 pandemic, forcing owners to revise menus and navigate heavy financial burdens.

items.title: India developing indigenous capability to detect unregistered virtual asset service providers: FATF report
items.summary: India is launching a groundbreaking Virtual Asset Lab to identify unregistered offshore virtual asset service providers. This initiative targets firms that exploit international boundaries to evade Indian regulations and facilitate money laundering. With this proactive measure, India is reinforcing its financial security and enhancing regulatory monitoring in the burgeoning realm of virtual assets.

items.title: FDI easing not for Chinese firms; to benefit entities with minority Chinese holding
items.summary: India has eased foreign investment rules. Overseas firms with up to ten percent Chinese shareholding can now invest automatically. Companies from China and nations sharing land borders with India will get faster approvals for investments in key manufacturing sectors. This aims to boost domestic production and attract foreign capital. The government is streamlining processes for greater certainty for investors.

items.title: Qualcomm Ventures eyes India startups to build low-cost AI for the world
items.summary: Qualcomm Ventures' new $150 million fund will back founders building lower-cost AI applications, robotics and edge technologies

items.title: Iran war ends soon? 'Practically nothing left' to target, claims Trump
items.summary: US President Donald Trump told Axios the war in Iran could end soon, claiming there is “practically nothing left” for US forces to target after weeks of strikes.

items.title: Campbell's cuts annual forecasts as demand sags, shares slump by 5%
items.summary: Campbell's has lowered its yearly sales and profit outlook. The company is also pausing share buybacks. This move prioritizes debt reduction. Weak consumer demand and rising costs are impacting sales. New tariffs are expected to increase expenses further. Campbell's aims to boost snack sales through promotions.

items.title: Wednesday wipeout: investor mood sours after Tuesday’s brief respite
items.summary: Renewed West Asia hostilities triggered a broad sell-off, erasing  ₹5.48 trillion in BSE market cap as benchmark indices hit 10-month lows. While oil-sensitive sectors face margin compression, analysts suggest current valuations offer long-term entry points despite rising volatility.

items.title: Retail inflation in Feb likely to have moved above 3%
items.summary: The India’s Statistics Ministry is set to release February's retail inflation data on Thursday

items.title: Govt cautions pharma cos against surrogate ads for promoting GLP-1 drugs
items.summary: Weight loss drug risks: The government has warned of strict regulatory action against those found using surrogate advertising to promote GLP-1 drugs. "A detailed advisory on the use of GLP-1 drugs is expected to be released in the next few days," sources told ET.

items.title: ED searches 19 locations in Haryana, Chandigarh in ₹590 crore IDFC First bank ‘fraud’ case
items.summary: The primary probe showed that huge funds have been transferred to the various shell companies and minor jewellery entities routed through various shell companies, say sources

items.title: Surge in LPG prices, supply crunch threaten livelihoods of auto and taxi drivers
items.summary: Even as auto and taxi drivers limit the number of rides to save on fuel, drivers on ride-hailing platforms say that they have not yet seen any disruption

items.summary: The launch comes as Ayodhya records a sharp rise in visitor numbers — from 57.5 million in 2023 to over 160 million in 2024, with more than 230 million devotees arriving in the first half of 2025 alone

items.summary: The fund invests primarily in equity shares of index constituents, with a small allocation retained in cash or short-term instruments for liquidity management

items.title: Buying gold or silver amid US–Iran conflict? Experts explain what to buy and how much to hold
items.summary: Rising tensions between the US and Iran have pushed investors back towards safe-haven assets like gold and silver amid growing geopolitical uncertainty. Experts explain what to buy and how much to hold.

items.title: IEA announces record release of strategic stocks in response to Iran war oil price surge
items.summary: The International Energy Agency has announced its largest-ever oil release of 400 million barrels to combat soaring crude prices, driven by supply disruptions from the U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran. This unprecedented collective action aims to prevent further price hikes amid fears of continued Iranian attacks blocking Middle East oil exports.

items.title: Retail investors flock to weekly options despite regulatory curbs and losses
items.summary: Combined average daily premium turnover (ADT) of index options such as Nifty and Sensex rose 5% even as combined cash market ADT dipped by almost 8% in the current fiscal through 9 March (FY26), from the same period a year ago.

items.title: Bengaluru eateries cut menu, business hours; govt warns black-marketers
items.summary: College hostels have also cut down on the use of cooking gas, avoiding deep fry items to stretch the existing stock for more days as supply situation remained precarious.

items.title: Induction cooktops, electric kettles sales surge amid LPG supply concerns
items.summary: Tata Group's Croma said it has observed a threefold jump in demand for induction cooktops over the past few days, while Stovekraft Ltd, the company behind the popular kitchen appliance brand Pigeon, noted that its average weekly online sales have jumped four times.

items.title: S Jaishankar discusses West Asia conflict with Russian counterpart
items.summary: Union External Affairs Minister Dr S Jaishankar spoke with his Russian counterpart on Wednesday, with both diplomats sharing their views on geopolitical concerns linked to the ongoing West Asia conflict.

items.title: NHAI-backed Raajmarg Infra InvIT IPO subscribed 16% on Day 1
items.summary: Raajmarg Infra Investment Trust's Rs 6,000-crore IPO saw 16% subscription on day one, with institutional investors showing stronger interest. The InvIT, backed by NHAI, aims to unlock highway asset potential and offers a long-term investment in five toll roads.  This public offering is a significant step towards broader participation in India's highway development.

items.title: Wall Street mixed after flat inflation data amid rising oil prices, Oracle surges 12.8%
items.summary: At 10:20 a.m. ET, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.45%, the S&amp;P 500 gained 0.10%, the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.48%

items.title: Nithin Kamath says genuine hedging in options market is now harder due to heavy speculation
items.summary: Zerodha co-founder Nithin Kamath highlighted a structural shift in India's options market, with a surge in weekly options trading making hedging difficult.  Trading has concentrated in ultra-short-term contracts, reducing liquidity in longer-dated options crucial for risk management, especially during geopolitical volatility.

items.title: OPEC confirms big Saudi oil production hike ahead of Iran war, holds forecasts steady
items.summary: Saudi Arabia significantly boosted oil production in February as a safeguard against potential disruptions from U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran. Despite this, OPEC maintained its forecast for robust global oil demand growth this year, while acknowledging the need to closely monitor geopolitical events. items.title: Oil falls further amid growing alarm over coronavirus second wave
items.summary: The biggest global health crisis in a century continues to flare unabated with cases rising in India, Great Britain, Spain and several parts of the United States, where the infection rate has not come under control for months.

items.title: Gold flat as dollar strength counters safe-haven demand
items.summary: Spot gold was little changed at $1,929.30 per ounce by 0038 GMT, after falling to a near two-week week low of $1,906.24 on Tuesday.

items.title: Gold may witness choppy trade, expect buying at lower levels: Kotak Securities
items.summary: After weeks of consolidation, crude oil has come under pressure amid signs of correction in US equity market and gains in US dollar index.

items.title: Energy Weekly Report: Motilal Oswal
items.summary: According to Motilal Oswal, Oil prices hit a rough patch last week, falling back in line with broader financial markets. Prices did try to inch higher, but experienced selling pressure at highs.

items.title: Precious Metals Weekly Report: Motilal Oswal
items.summary: According to Motilal Oswal, Gold fell to the lowest level in a month primarily as the dollar rose against its major crosses and better-than-expected economic number from the US.

items.title: Base Metals Weekly Report: Motilal Oswal
items.summary: According to Motilal Oswal, Base metals traded choppy last week, with prices hitting multi month highs at the start of the week and followed by some profit taking on back of volatility in the dollar and mixed data signals from China and US.

items.title: Flipkart records highest-ever single day sale, grosses $200mn
items.summary: This is the biggest ever single day sale for Bangalore-based Flipkart, since the time the company started in 2007.

items.title: Subramanian Swamy seeks ban on import of cement from Pakistan
items.summary: "Allowing imports of cement from Pakistan, therefore, carried with it the additional risk in that it provides an effective cover for smuggling of contraband goods and harmful weapons and ammunition concealed in cement bags which comes in rakes and trucks, in the hands of disruptionist elements," he said.

items.title: Moneycontrol Exclusive: Govt plans overhaul of tax administration for smooth GST rollout
items.summary: The government has readied plans for a major change in India’s tax administration structure, including overhauling the Central Board of Excise and Customs (CBEC) to ensure a glitch-free roll-out of a nation-wide goods and services tax (GST).

items.title: Sensex, Nifty wobbly; Hind Zinc, SBI, Force Motors most active
items.summary: Asian Paints, SBI, Tata Motors, HUL and Maruti Suzuki are top gainers while ONGC, Axis Bank, MM, Bajaj Auto and Hero MotoCorp are major losers in the Sensex.

items.title: Uber  redBus partner with ixigo for train travellers
items.summary: ixigo, India’s leading mobile travel marketplace has announced partnerships with Uber and redBus to enable instant bookings for cabs and buses natively within ixigo trains app.

items.title: Workload in IT to come down due to AI; jobs to reduce: Genpact
items.summary: But along with that the employment growth rates have started to dip, NV 'Tiger' Tyagarajan says. The percentage addition of employees in India will not be same as past. And due to advancements, a workforce with higher skill sets is required for the IT industry, he says.

items.title: Pixel launch: Is Google Assistant's AI an assault on privacy?
items.summary: Sure, artificial intelligence can make life simpler by suggesting you places to eat, tell you weather, or even crack a joke.  The more you use it, the more it learns about your habits and preferences. The protocols pertaining to artificial intelligence, or the lack of it, make our data less secure and more vulnerable.

items.title: Up over 200% in 1 year, what's buzzing with Borosil Glass?
items.summary: Shares of Borosil Glass Works have increased threefold over the past one year as the company has posted strong revenue and EBIDTA growth over the past few quarters.

items.title: Why you should not be always chasing returns
items.summary: Most people focus on returns and chase products that offer the best returns at any point.

items.title: Jhunjhunwala-partnered fund buys stake in TFCI: What's buzzing?
items.summary: In an interview with CNBC-TV18, Satpal Arora, MD of Tourism Finance Corporation of India (TFCI), talked about the state of business and the outlook ahead.

items.title: RBI latest meeting 'diluted' its old policy framework: Nomura
items.summary: On Tuesday, the central bank surprised the market with a 25-basis-point cut to its policy repo rate, or the rate at which it lends to banks, to 6.25 percent.

items.title: Bond bull market may pause but is far from over: Expert
items.summary: The benchmark 10-year government-security yield remained stuck in 8-7.5 percent range through all of 2015 and half of 2016, moving lower to sub-7 percent only when the RBI promised in April to reduce the system's liquidity deficit. The yield may now fall more.

items.title: Scope for meaningful rate cuts going ahead: Neelkanth Mishra
items.summary: Credit Suisse' Neelkanth Mishra expects repo rate to fall to a decade low in the coming quarters. He says that beginning December the market may see a robust and widespread pick-up which may boost indices.

items.title: Tech up to a healthier world
items.summary: Sensors, 3D printed skin, wearables, reconstructed organs, revolutionized clinical trials, personalized medicine and what not – get ready for a new era of healthcare

items.title: Steel stocks rally as govt extends MIP on 66 steel products
items.summary: Stocks like Hindustan Zinc, Hindalco, Jindal Steel, JSW Steel and Tata Steel gained over 1 percent from previous close.

items.title: Endurance Technologies IPO opens: Should you subscribe?
items.summary: The Aurangabad-based company aims to mop up nearly Rs 1,162 crore (at higher end of price band) by diluting 17.5 percent stake through the issue that will close on October 7. It already raised Rs 348.52 crore through anchor investors' portion on Tuesday, the day before issue opening.

items.title: Cautiously optimistic on India; eyeing earnings revival: Sanger
items.summary: Global liquidity tailwinds are unlikely to get any better from now and that makes Arvind Sanger, Managing Partner at Geosphere Capital Management “cautiously optimistic” on the Indian markets.

items.title: Automation threatens 69% jobs in India: World Bank
items.summary: "In large parts of Africa, it is likely that technology could fundamentally disrupt this pattern. Research based on World Bank data has predicted that the proportion of jobs threatened in India by automation is 69 percent, in China it is 77 percent and in Ethiopia, the percentage of jobs threatened by automation is 85 percent," he said.

items.title: Young savers drift away even as retirees prop up bank deposits
items.summary: Although banks losing depositors to other avenues is not new, fresh RBI data sheds light on how young Indians are no longer reliant on banks

items.title: Why Indian kitchens need to quit gas and go electric: In war or peace, it’ll serve us better
items.summary: The Iran war has spotlighted India’s reliance on imported energy—even for something as basic as cooking meals. A shift from LPG and PNG to efficient induction stoves could cushion us. With a few reforms, India could go in for a climate-friendly electric transition.

items.title: What explains upmarket Apple’s low-priced MacBook? A chip crunch more than market opportunity
items.summary: Apple has long guarded its premium aura, but slowing laptop sales and a global chip shortage may have forced a rethink. Its low-priced MacBook aimed at students aims to mop up sales in a high-volume segment even as a supply chain squeeze threatens high-end sales this year.

items.title: West Asia crisis: Indian airlines seek pilot duty rule relief as longer routes breach flight limits
items.summary: Longer routings after Pakistan airspace closure and West Asia tensions have pushed some international flights beyond the 10-hour limit for two-pilot crews.

items.title: Uncertainty amid the Iran War: Why the answers to India’s external troubles lie squarely at home
items.summary: As war in West Asia drives up oil prices and geopolitical uncertainty, India’s exposure to external risks has grown. In response, we need a renewed focus on sound economic policy, private sector investment and job generation.

items.title: US‑Israel war with Iran: Crude spikes, rupee crashes, airspace shuts — Indian airlines stare at  ₹180 bn losses
items.summary: Ongoing conflict in West Asia has plunged India's aviation sector into crisis, leading to over 1,100 flight cancellations and rising operational costs. The closure of crucial airspaces and a depreciating rupee are pressuring airlines, with estimated losses reaching  ₹170–180 billion.

items.title: US-Israel’s Iran War blitz: Has China decided to hide its strength and bide its time?
items.summary: China’s Gulf diplomacy could end up under the rubble of the Iran war even as its economy defies revival plans. Beijing has scaled back its GDP target. But what about its global power ambitions? There’s no sign of anything but a tactical pullback.

items.title: US-Israeli attack on Iran: Under what conditions can an illegal war claim moral legitimacy?
items.summary: Armed aggression that violates international law can sometimes claim moral legitimacy. History offers rare examples. But does the US-Israeli attack on Iran meet that test—or does it instead reveal how far today’s leaders are willing to discard both law and legitimacy?

items.title: Theatres empty as Ramzan, exams, sports wreak havoc
items.summary: Ramzan, school exams, and the T20 World Cup have severely impacted the movie industry, leading to delayed releases. Anticipation builds for Dhurandhar: The Revenge, expected to revive box-office fortunes, as producers remain cautious amid a lack of appealing content.

items.title: The odds of financial crises are rising. Will the US be there to help?
items.summary: The U.S. historically leads recovery efforts. That is becoming less certain, Robert Hormats writes in a guest commentary.

items.title: The Third Gulf War is raising plumes of smoke but could this appalling war have a silver lining?
items.summary: Iran’s Hormuz chokehold shows how easily a war in West Asia can rattle global energy markets. But perhaps this oil shock could do what years of climate talks have not: jolt governments out of complacency and hasten the world’s shift to cleaner options.

items.title: The OTT paradox: More shows than ever, but fewer real hits
items.summary: Data from Ormax shows a handful of Indian OTT originals dominate viewership while most titles struggle for attention, highlighting growing audience polarization on streaming platforms.

items.title: Telecom sector asks govt for priority fuel, power supply amid West Asia crisis
items.summary: An industry body representing these companies has written to the government seeking priority electricity supply for mobile tower sites and an exemption for telecom tower manufacturing units from a recent order restricting LPG supply.

items.title: Telcos, tech firms clash over India's enterprise internet
items.summary: Trai is reviewing decade-old price caps on business internet lines, pitting telecom giants against tech firms over market fairness and transparency. The outcome could change how enterprise connectivity is priced and accessed nationwide.

items.title: Scammed? RBI will pay you back, but only once in a lifetime
items.summary: RBI has proposed a one-time policy to reimburse victims of small-value digital fraud, even in cases of customer negligence. The cost-sharing model between the central bank and lenders seeks to bolster digital trust.

items.title: SBI rolls out $500 million social loan programme for women empowerment
items.summary: State Bank of India has launched a USD 500 million syndicated social term loan focused on women empowerment, marking a significant step in ESG financing.&nbsp;

items.title: RBI relaxes dividend rules for banks, keeps capital discipline intact
items.summary: The most significant modification is about how bad loans are treated while calculating profits available for dividend distribution. In the draft proposal issued on 6 January, banks were required to deduct 100% of their net non-performing assets from profit to arrive at adjusted profit after tax.

items.title: Prudent path: India should stay resolutely neutral as wars erupt in a world without rules
items.summary: India has stakes across the world’s geopolitical ruptures—as evident in Europe, West Asia and potentially even East Asia. In such a dangerously fractured world, the wisest course is strategic aloofness: focus on managing the fallout of wars while keeping a safe distance.

items.title: Promoters sell apartment at Raheja Artesia in Worli for  ₹121 crore
items.summary: Mumbai's Worli sees another mega apartment sale at  ₹1.7 lakh per sq ft as Ravi Raheja, group president of real estate firm K Raheja Corp and his wife sell apartment at Raheja Artesia

items.title: Planning an FD? Check latest interest rates of SBI, HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, and other top lenders in India
items.summary: Fixed deposits provide guaranteed returns with higher interest rates than savings accounts, appealing to risk-averse investors. Here's a comparison between the major banks in India.

items.title: PM review asks DoT to check if data can be taxed; wants BSNL to trim staff
items.summary: India is considering a tax on data usage as a means to generate government revenue and also a model to influence screen time, especially among children. In a review meeting, DoT has been given time until September 2026 for this. Separately, the meeting called for efforts to reduce BSNL staff.

items.title: Operation Epic Fury sparks a high-stakes balancing act in India
items.summary: As the West Asian conflict intensifies, New Delhi navigates a precarious diplomatic path to safeguard vital energy supplies and millions of overseas citizens.

items.title: Mint Quick Edit | Mutual fund investor interest in equities revived last month: Will the Gulf war hurt it?
items.summary: Retail investors may be rediscovering their appetite for equities, with inflows into mid- and small-cap funds surging in February. But fresh uncertainty looms: the Gulf war’s oil shock. Will inflows slow?

items.title: Mint Quick Edit | End of Iran’s regime? Why prediction markets generate both information and anxiety
items.summary: Bets on crystal-ball platforms like Polymarket over war outcomes and the like reveal what people think. That makes them useful. But what about insider trading? And perverse incentives to make ‘killings’ in such prediction markets?

items.title: Mint Quick Edit | China’s latest retail inflation number may be a relief but deflation still haunts its economy
items.summary: China’s inflation ticked up in February after its flat reading in January, but we can’t infer that its campaign against ‘involution’—excessive rivalry—is working. Perhaps it needs a big dose of fiscal stimulus to overcome its price-softening woes.

items.title: Mint Explainer| Will the US-Iran war nudge India towards bio-bitumen for road construction?
items.summary: Bio-bitumen, an alternative derived from biomass, could replace up to a third of petroleum-based bitumen, potentially reducing India’s import dependence amid the West Asia conflict.

items.title: Mint Explainer: Can companies challenge the government’s LNG diversions in court?
items.summary: While the union government is using its emergency powers to block lawsuits over LNG diversions to priority sectors, legal experts say constitutional courts still hold the power to strike down orders that are arbitrary or unreasonable.

items.title: Mint Explainer | Why Indian restaurants are raising the alarm over LPG supply
items.summary: Industry bodies say a government order prioritizing LPG for households has created confusion over commercial cylinder supply used by restaurants.

items.title: Mint Explainer | RBI’s digital fraud compensation proposal: Who will get refunds and how it will work
items.summary: The central bank wants banks, customers and the regulator to share the burden of small-value digital fraud losses.

items.title: Mint Explainer | Direct seeded rice saves water and costs. What’s holding back farmers?
items.summary: As groundwater levels decline, labour costs rise and climate plays havoc, policymakers and scientists are increasingly promoting direct seeded rice (DSR) as a more viable alternative to traditional rice cultivation. However, there are stumbling blocks hindering its wider adoption.

items.title: Meta cautions Karnataka over under-16s' social media ban: Curbs may 'push teens toward less safe, unregulated sites'
items.summary: Meta Platforms stated that it will follow domestic regulations but noted that, with teens using roughly 40 apps weekly, focusing on a few companies won't ensure their safety.

items.title: Marketers, take note: The spending patterns of Indian households change in distinct ways as incomes go up
items.summary: Nobody can bank on assumptions of linear growth in spending by Indian households as they earn more. As a survey reveals, different income brackets display distinct patterns—with new market categories entering the picture—as key thresholds are crossed.

items.title: Manu Joseph: Artificial intelligence isn’t funny and seems unable to grasp why that’s so
items.summary: AI can write essays, poems and code, but ask it to be funny and it fails. Ask it why it isn’t funny and it fails again. What it doesn’t get is that humour is a form of human excellence—and that’s rare even among humans.

items.title: Lala Shri Ram: How a failed entrepreneur built one of India's industrial dynasties
items.summary: For a man who arrived in his family business without any credentials, Lala Shri Ram left behind two of India’s best colleges, a research institute, and one of independent India's largest industrial houses.

items.title: Jack Dorsey shouldn’t scare people: Every employer needn’t deploy AI to lay human workers off
items.summary: Fears of artificial intelligence gobbling up jobs rise each time it enables layoffs—as with Jack Dorsey’s Block. But whether AI replaces or empowers workers may depend on the choices made by companies and governments. Pro-worker AI may be possible.

items.title: Iran War: Trump’s secretary of state may have let the bloodcurdling truth slip
items.summary: The Trump administration’s messaging of America's role in the Iran war is confusing at best and chilling at worst. The secretary of state's remark implying Trump followed Netanyahu’s lead exposes not only a lack of sovereign leadership, but inconsistencies in the White House view of US alliances

items.title: India’s data could hold the key to keeping frontier AI models accessible in a world of uncertainty
items.summary: Frontier AI is racing ahead, but a handful of tech firms control access to the most powerful models. As geopolitical flux raises the risk of tech-denial regimes taking hold, India should use its vast data troves as a bargaining chip to strike AI-access deals. This window won’t stay open for too long

items.title: Indian refiners are buying both sanctioned and non-sanctioned Russian oil following US waiver
items.summary: On Friday, US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Washington would allow a 30-day waiver for Indian refiners to purchase Russian oil stranded at sea, offering temporary relief amid global supply concerns.

items.title: India turns the page: Publishing boom opens new chapter for books
items.summary: Publishers and bookstore owners are a happy lot today. Because one thing has become clear at lit fests and book fairs from Chumoukedima to Banaras, Shillong to Hyderabad: India’s book business is booming.

items.title: India targets chip design self-reliance, eyes Indian MNC talent
items.summary: Niti Aayog's Vijay Kumar Saraswat said that Indian designers contribute to virtually every chip produced globally. However, their work is primarily for multinational companies, and they are often limited to designing subsystems or specific components rather than complete system designs.

items.title: India should aim to be the world’s largest agri and processed food exporter: Goyal
items.summary: Commerce minister Piyush Goyal said deeper collaboration and value addition can help India aim for global leadership in agri exports.

items.title: In the works—digital approvals, tighter entry norms for foreign airlines
items.summary: India may soon require foreign airlines to obtain operating authorization through a fully digital approval system. Draft rules from regulator DGCA propose standardized documentation, a local representative and stricter checks on ownership, safety and regulatory compliance.

items.title: Hormuz is just one of many chokepoints that are pushing countries to rethink economic strategy
items.summary: The Cold War-style weaponization of supply bottlenecks—from the Strait of Hormuz to rare-earth clamps—is forcing countries to rework their strategies in favour of self-sufficiency as geopolitics makes global interdependence harder by the day.

items.title: Have you noticed your AI assistant’s favourite words?
items.summary: Chatbots tend to overuse some words until they’ve become the fingerprints that identify AI-produced content.

items.title: Gold-dollar dynamics: What the US ‘ashvamedha’ run and AI could mean global for investors
items.summary: Investors betting on gold’s rise and the dollar’s decline as a reserve currency may need to rethink their assumptions. The Iran war has seen the dollar strengthen and gold fall. Will this last? Here are four scenarios for investors around the world to track.

items.title: German media group Axel Springer to buy publisher of UK's Daily Telegraph for $766 million
items.summary: The deal ends a long saga over ownership of the Telegraph Media Group, which publishes the 171-year-old Daily Telegraph and its Sunday sister paper

items.title: Gas crunch pushes govt to prioritize city supply, curb industry allocations
items.summary: The government has ordered full supply for households and CNG vehicles while cutting allocations to fertilizer, refineries and industry after disruptions in Qatar and the Strait of Hormuz push up global LNG prices.

items.title: From bidets to AI labs: Japan’s hidden innovators should step out and learn to play the hype game
items.summary: Many Japanese companies are proving remarkably innovative and thriving off it in the age of AI. Yet their reluctance to trumpet their success often leaves investors in the dark. About time they learnt a trick or two in the art of hype.

items.title: From 'Stranger Things' to UEFA: India's courts arm content owners with ‘dynamic’ protection against online piracy
items.summary: Dynamic plus injunctions aim to reduce the time lag between discovery and blocking of pirate sites, offering critical protection for high-value initial releases. However, enforcement faces challenges due to jurisdictional issues and the adaptability of piracy networks.

items.title: Freebies galore: The 16th Finance Commission warns against them and so does ancient history
items.summary: The 16th Finance Commission has echoed its predecessors in warning about the harsh fiscal fallout of unchecked freebies. But subsidies and cash transfers have a way of veering out of control. It’s a lesson that an emperor of olden times learnt a little too late.

items.title: Factories to the fore: India must reboot its growth model before the AI-pocalypse hits its IT sector
items.summary: India’s IT services boom drive exports, employment and middle-class growth for three decades plus. But with agentic AI out to automate much of what the sector does, that success story may be nearing its limits. It’s time to turbo-charge manufacturing—which needs a renaissance.

items.title: Event prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket are buzzing in America: Should India allow them too?
items.summary: Prediction markets on platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi are witnessing a boom, with wagers being made on outcomes of war, elections, sports and more. Their appeal is undeniable—even as questions swirl over regulation and insider information.

items.title: Equal opportunities for women may be the fastest way to generate jobs and accelerate economic growth
items.summary: Well-crafted laws that empower women can significantly lift an economy’s performance. Yet, in much of the world, legal rights exist largely on paper, with weak enforcement and missing support systems keeping millions out of work. Closing this gap could unlock jobs, entrepreneurship and faster growth

items.title: Devina Mehra: How hard do wars hit stock markets? What the historical record suggests
items.summary: Every major conflict sparks fears and market turmoil. Yet, the past half century (or more), from the two earlier Gulf wars to the US bombing of Libya, shows that conflicts have no lasting impact on stock markets. While they get shaken, they recover soon enough.

items.title: Deploy municipal bonds to give urban India the makeover it needs—Here’s how to attract investors
items.summary: Urban local bodies (ULBs) must mobilize vast sums to modernize India’s rapidly expanding cities and keep them liveable. Municipal bonds are a useful way to raise that capital. But India must first strengthen the market for ULB debt. Here’s what could be done.

items.title: Data, design and dosa batter: How Instamart is turning your 10-min craving into a private label empire
items.summary: Private labels are usually invisible and price-led. But Swiggy Instamart wants them to be visible and aspirational. By blending hyperlocal data with bold design, Swiggy’s Instamart is trying to build an FMCG food and beverages brand within its delivery app ecosystem.

items.title: DCGI cracks down on indirect promotion of weight-loss prescription drugs
items.summary: This prohibition shall extend to any form of direct or indirect promotional activity in print, electronic, digital, social media, or any other public platform that is intended, directly or indirectly, to promote the product to the general public.&nbsp;

items.title: Clive Crook: Just how resilient is the US economy? The Iran war could turn out to be a test
items.summary: Inflation risk, tariff turmoil and a brazen fiscal overstretch were already testing the US economy. The fallout of the Iran war now adds uncertainty that could push an indebted America to an economic reckoning should hostilities refuse to follow its script.

items.title: Clean fuel confusion prompts part-makers to chart tech-agnostic path
items.summary: As global carmakers pivot away from an EV-only strategy, Indian part-makers are shifting toward ‘powertrain-agnostic’ portfolios that support electric, hybrid, and internal combustion platforms simultaneously. The strategic hedge allows suppliers to remain resilient in changing times.

items.title: Centre prioritizes domestic LPG supply as oil price surge raises supply risks
items.summary: India has taken decisive steps to prioritize household LPG access amid escalating tensions in West Asia that threaten fuel supplies. With the introduction of a 25-day lock-in period for cylinder bookings, the government aims to curb hoarding and ensure domestic consumers are prioritized.

items.title: Battle of pipelines: Hormuz is clamped but other routes exist for Gulf oil to flow—if Iran lets it
items.summary: Global oil markets may stabilize if even a modest share of Gulf supply can avoid the clamped Strait of Hormuz. Saudi Arabia and the UAE are activating bypass pipelines to reroute exports. But Iran’s gameplan is to worsen today’s oil shock.

items.title: Bank holidays: Are banks closed today and tomorrow on 7-8 March for the weekend? Check full RBI calendar here
items.summary: The RBI has listed a total of 18 public holidays for all banks in March 2026 for public and private lenders across India. This also includes the weekend holidays for second Saturday, fourth Saturday, and all Sundays this month.

items.title: As oil soars and stocks slide, investors in India should focus on equity yields over capital gains
items.summary: The Iran war has made oil spike and equities slide. While RBI could steady the rupee with its dollar reserves, investors should focus on what stocks are actually worth instead of what they can be encashed for. Equity yields over capital gains, that is.

items.title: Are actors obligated to promote their films? Why marketing is now written into contracts
items.summary: As competition from OTT platforms intensifies, producers are increasingly making promotional appearances a contractual obligation for actors.

items.title: Anthropic’s warning raises urgent questions about the use of AI for mass surveillance by the state
items.summary: Anthropic’s clash with the Pentagon has brought into focus a troubling prospect: AI could help the state stitch together scattered digital traces and surveil citizens at scale. This power raises questions about privacy, freedom and the future of dissent in every democracy.

items.title: Ajit Ranade: India’s stubbornly high PF payout rate is getting in the way of its monetary policy
items.summary: The EPFO’s 8.25% payout is 3 percentage points above the Reserve Bank of India’s policy rate. For RBI’s credit easing to take effect, the PF rate must be linked in some way with rates in the larger savings market. When will India’s political economy let this distortion end?

items.title: Airlines knock on government’s door as West Asia war extends, seek relief for higher operating costs, says report
items.summary: Indian airlines have requested the government for relief due to increased operational costs and flight disruptions caused by the West Asia conflict. Here's what we know and the latest status update on flight cancellations, routes for 9 March.

items.title: After 48 years, India revises piston ring standards to curb carbon emissions
items.summary: The new standards replace specifications from 1977 and aim to align India's automobile market with global efficiency and emission benchmarks.

items.title: A guilt gap in Indian kitchens is a $50 bn market for clean-label ingredients. E.g.: idli, dosa batter
items.summary: The Indian kitchen faces a conflict between modern convenience and traditional values, leading to an 85% adoption gap in the ready-to-eat market. Successful brands will enable cooking while respecting tradition, creating a culinary experience for consumers who seek engagement over replacement.

items.title: A 20-year trip from 7-day failure to long-term rule
items.summary: The question remains; will Nishant be able to fill in the boots left by his father? The other question is who would control the government in Bihar?
